seo.title: Valentin Alekhin — Senior Full-stack Developer
seo.description: I design and build SaaS, web applications, and internal systems—from idea to launch.

hero.statement: I build web products from idea to launch.
hero.description: I design and build SaaS, web applications, and internal systems—without gaps between design, frontend, and backend.

projects.title: Products built around real workflows
projects.description: Not a gallery of screens, but systems where interface, architecture, and launch work as one.

powersketch.summary: A browser-based service for designing electrical distribution boards.
powersketch.description: PowerSketch helps electricians and smart-home integrators assemble distribution-board projects without complex desktop software—from device placement to connection reports and link sharing.
powersketch.challenge: The product had to combine the precision of an engineering tool with an approachable web interface. A large device catalog, scalable canvas, pin connections, and project data all needed to stay consistent.
powersketch.role: I own the product end to end: workflows, interface and data-model design, frontend, backend, and continued product development after launch.
powersketch.approach: The editor is centered on an independent SVG scene. Devices, wires, layers, and annotations are data, so the same project can be edited, stored, copied, and exported without divergent representations.
powersketch.architecture: The interactive client is separated from server responsibilities. The backend handles authentication, projects, the device library, public access, and subscriptions, while the UI optimizes frequent canvas operations and synchronizes durable project state.
powersketch.result: A working browser product with a drag-and-drop editor, smart connections, layers, a device catalog, reports, shared access, and paid plans. Unverified user or financial metrics are deliberately excluded.
powersketch.constraints: The key trade-offs are large-scene SVG performance, export fidelity, backward compatibility of stored projects, and predictable editor behavior across screen sizes.

capabilities.title: From an uncertain idea to a working system
capabilities.description: I can own the complete product or a specific layer while keeping user workflows and technical decisions connected.

items.title: Discovery / Product
items.description: I clarify the problem, constraints, and first-release scope before optimizing delivery.

items.title: Interface

items.title: Frontend

items.title: Backend / Integrations

items.title: Launch

process.title: A transparent process without extra layers
process.description: You communicate directly with the person making product and technical decisions and accountable for the result.

items.title: Discussion
items.description: We unpack the problem, users, constraints, and what a useful outcome means.

items.title: Proposal
items.description: I define the solution, scope, stages, timeline, and a suitable engagement model.

items.title: Development
items.description: Work moves in short stages with regular demonstrations and feedback.

items.title: Launch
items.description: I verify production scenarios, deploy the product, and stay available after release.

about.title: Interface and architecture are parts of the same decision
paragraphs: I work full-stack because product decisions rarely stop at the edge of a layout. Interface state depends on the data model, while a dependable launch depends on architecture and operations.
I prefer work where I can understand the real workflow, test the direction with a prototype, and carry it through to actual use—not merely hand off files.
principles: I do not separate interface from architecture.
I prefer a working prototype to a long document.
I automate anything I have to do twice.
A product is only finished after launch.

contact.title: Have a task or a product idea?
contact.description: Tell me what you are building, what already works, and where you need technical help. A short note is enough to begin.
contact.dialogDescription: Choose the easiest way to start the conversation.
